g.title: Completed View of the Assembled Kit
g.desc: An isometric drawing of the fully assembled device. It is a tall, shield-shaped object made from two layers of 5mm plywood. The front features a large 8 by 8 grid of small square holes in the center. Three brass screws are visible: one on the right side near the top of the grid and two at the bottom corners. At the very bottom, there are two small square openings, one of which is marked with a capital letter E.

g.title: Project Component Layout and Identification
g.desc: This component guide shows several parts to organize: a large wooden Front Panel with a large central square opening and tapered top; a wooden Back Panel with two vertical rectangular cutouts; and a trapezoidal Kickstand with a central cross-shaped slot. Electronic parts include a square LED Matrix with a tactile grid and a flat ribbon Jumper Wire. Small items include two rectangular Foam Tape strips, four 5/8-inch brass screws, and four small hexagonal brass nuts.

g.title: Large Wooden Front Panel with Square Cutout
g.desc: A single 5mm thick plywood piece with a tombstone-like shape: a flat bottom with rounded corners and a top that tapers inward. It features a large square cutout in the center. To orient the piece, find the top where the corners are angled inward. Above the central square cutout is a small circular hole. Along the bottom edge, you will find two more small holes with the word 'PIPER' engraved between them.

g.title: Five-Pin Jumper Wire Ribbon Cable
g.desc: This component is a long, flexible ribbon consisting of five thin wires joined side-by-side. You can identify it tactually as a flat, multi-strand cable. One end terminates in a single wide, rectangular plastic connector housing with five small pins or holes arranged in a single row. The other end terminates in individual plugs with 5 individual metal pins and small plastic housings.

g.title: Identify the 8x8 LED Matrix Module
g.desc: A square electronic module with an 8 by 8 grid of 64 tiny, slightly raised square lights on its front face. To orient the piece, find the five thin metal pins protruding from one edge on the back side.

g.title: Back Panel Component Identification
g.desc: This is a single large, flat wooden piece called the Back Panel. It has a complex shape: the bottom is a straight horizontal edge with two small screw holes near the corners. The top edge is irregular, featuring a square shoulder on the left and a taller, rounded 'thumb-like' protrusion on the right that contains a small screw hole. Between these two top features is a deep U-shaped notch. In the center of the panel, there are two large identical vertical rectangular cutouts. Between these large rectangles, there is a small vertical slot with a screw hole below it. Another small vertical slot is located near the center of the bottom edge.

g.title: Single Trapezoidal Wood Kickstand Component
g.desc: This is a single flat, trapezoidal plywood piece. Its top edge is shorter than the bottom edge. On the bottom edge, you can feel two wide, rectangular feet at the corners. In the center of the bottom edge is a vertical slot that opens into a cross-shaped cutout. To orient it, place the side with the two feet and the cross-shaped cutout facing towards you.

g.title: Locate Two Foam Tape Strips
g.desc: Find two identical pieces of foam tape. These are thin, flexible, rectangular strips. Unlike the rigid plywood or plastic pieces, they will feel soft and compressible. Each strip will have a smooth paper or plastic backing on one side that can be peeled away to reveal a sticky surface. You will notice that each strip is significantly longer than it is wide. Once these are adhered to the RGB matrix PCBA, they should not be removed.

g.title: Four 5/8-inch Brass Screws
g.desc: Locate four identical screws made of brass. Each screw measures 5/8 of an inch long. Tactilely, feel for a rounded, slightly domed head at one end of the screw. On top of this head, you may feel a cross-shaped indentation. The body of the screw is a slender shaft covered in spiraling ridges, called threads. You can confirm its length by holding the head and feeling the threaded shaft extend 5/8 of an inch.

g.title: Locate Four #4-40 Hex Nuts
g.desc: Find four small, metallic brass nuts. Each nut feels like a tiny, short cylinder. When you trace your finger around its outer edge, you'll feel six flat sides, making it a hexagon shape. In the center of the nut, you can feel a round hole with spiral ridges inside, which is where a #4-40 bolt will screw in.
